images.altText: Donald Trump speaking at a podium in the White House Press Briefing room.

children.content: US President Donald Trump is holding a press conference with military officials at the White House to discuss the war in the Middle East. Follow live.

items.title: Tech starts to eat itself as AI reshapes workforce

children.content: The wave of change from artificial intelligence rolling across the corporate landscape is going largely unnoticed as attention understandably shifts to an escalating war.

items.title: 'MAGA for Mamdani': The issue flipping Trump voters to a left-wing Democrat

children.content: The US president and New York mayor Zohran Mamdani are from polar opposite ends of the political spectrum, but comic Ron Barba voted for them both.

image.altText: A man in a MAGA for Mamdani t-shirt and hat.

images.altText: A male astronaut's head is seen in profile, looking back at Earth through a spacecraft cabin window.

children.content: The Artemis II astronauts have travelled further from Earth than any humans before. The crew is now on a historic six-hour flyby of the Moon, capturing never-before-seen views of its far side. Follow live.

items.title: Artemis II breaks distance record, Moon 'bright spot' named after commander's late wife

updates.title: Will they see previous Apollo landing sites?
Why Artemis II isn't landing on the Moon
Artemis II captures never-before-seen views of the Moon

items.title: Pro-climate son-in-law to Rupert Murdoch backs Farrer independent

children.content: Backers of Farrer independent candidate Michelle Milthorpe are celebrating after securing the support of a prominent pro-climate farmer who is also the son-in-law of media mogul Rupert Murdoch.

image.altText: Michelle Milthorpe talks to two people with their backs to the camera in the bistro of a pub.

items.title: Israeli settler violence in the West Bank deepens against backdrop of Iran war

children.content: Under the cover of regional conflict, Israelis who have established illegal settlements and outposts in the West Bank become emboldened to take over more land as world attention remains on the war in Iran.

image.altText: Mourners sit outside a hospital morgue in the West Bank.

items.title: How China uses ferries and fishing boats to pressure Taiwan

children.content: China quietly mobilised thousands of fishing boats twice in recent months to form floating barriers at least 300 kilometres long.

image.altText: A graphic features fishing vessels, a target and maps featuring the flags of China and Taiwan.

items.title: 'No one else should go through this': Young job hunters targets in scam surge

children.content: Job seekers should be wary of sharing their bank account information with potential employers, as job scams targeting young people surge, according to Scamwatch.

image.altText: A man with a brown hoodie is scrolling on his phone.

items.title: Zein speaks broken Arabic — here's how she made peace with that

children.content: For Heywire winner Zein, her family is remembered in the fragments she remembers of Syria.

items.title: Tropical Cyclone Maila likely to cross Far North Queensland

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology is increasingly confident the system will move towards the Queensland coast later in the week, potentially crossing Cape York Peninsula.

image.altText: A satellite image of a large amount of cloud cover in a circle on a map

items.title: A luxury beef giant's cattle died of thirst. Regulators were told later

children.content: The lack of investigation into deaths at the $700 million beef business has been described as "completely unacceptable" by a legal expert who argued industry had captured government regulators.

image.altText: A herd of AACo's wagyu heifers at Westholme station in Queensland.

items.title: The first world country in the grip of a poverty crisis

children.content: Once a mighty empire, Britain is now a broken kingdom. A staggering one fifth of the nation lives in poverty and that number is rising.

items.title: 'Are you sure?': First Nations voters asked about identity at polling booths

children.content: South Australia's Aboriginal Affairs Minister will consider an independent review of the state's Voice to Parliament
election, following claims some voters were queried about their racial background at polling booths, while others were forced to queue for hours.

image.altText: A woman with long brown hair wearing a white t-shirt with the words "LAND BACK" and an Aboriginal flag and a Palestinian flag.

items.title: Ballarat's unique approach to tackling family violence

children.content: Ballarat's approach targets causes of domestic and family violence as rates remain high.

image.altText: A wall with little photos of women's faces on it.

items.title: Hidden costs mean many comedians make 'zero profit' from festival runs

children.content: Doing a run of shows at the Melbourne International Comedy Festival is the pinnacle for a lot of Australian comedians, but the festival's tens of thousands of punters are rarely privy to the behind-the-scenes costs.

items.title: No Bluesfest? No worries as stars come out to wow Byron Easter crowds

children.content: There was no Bluesfest, but thousands of music fans still packed venues across the Byron Shire over the Easter long weekend.

image.altText: A concert with lots of people holding up their phones

items.title: Murray River ski boat launches into tree as race puts four in hospital

children.content: Flips, spin-outs and crashes have sent four competitors to hospital as the world's fastest water ski race was held without skiers for the second straight year after fatalities in the sport in 2024.

image.altText: Dozens of people on water ski boats at a river bank, with the Murray River in the background

items.title: Chinese AI helping Iran target US forces with 'incredible precision'

children.content: Chinese satellite images enhanced by artificial intelligence could help Iran target US and allied forces to within a third of a square metre, military analysts say.

image.altText: An aerial view of land.

items.title: Australia's fuel shipments secured 'well into' May

children.content: Fuel shipments to Australia have been secured "well into" May, the energy minister says, as the number of service stations with diesel and petrol shortages fell over the Easter weekend.

image.altText: A close-up shot of Chris Bowen, looking serious and wearing a suit and tie.

items.title: Outback tourist pub had two visitors this Easter amid fuel price pinch

children.content: Just two tourists have stopped at the iconic Pink Roadhouse during the Easter weekend, as travellers reconsidered their Easter weekend plans due to rising fuel costs.

image.altText: A road house sign at dusk

items.title: Aussie stars testing the waters at Australian Open

children.content: Molly O'Callaghan and Kyle Chalmers claimed Australian Open gold medals in the 100m freestyle events, while Sam Short showed his 400m rivals a clean pair of heels on night one at the Gold Coast Aquatic Centre.

items.title: 'I hate points': Gunston's last-second behind helps Hawthorn claim thriller

children.content: Hawthorn's Jack Gunston kicks a behind in the dying seconds to defeat Geelong by one point in an Easter Monday classic.

image.altText: Jack Gunston hugs Mitch Lewis after Hawthorn's win

items.title: Marshall questions penalty against Tigers despite Eels star's ugly ankle injury

children.content: As the Eels grapple with another potentially season-ending injury to Bailey Simonsson, Wests Tigers coach Benji Marshall says he is not even sure the tackle that led to it was worth a penalty.

image.altText: Luke Laulilii of the Wests Tigers is shown off the field by referee Peter Gough during an NRL game.

items.title: The unexpected place new collectors are finding affordable art

children.content: Horse drawings and handcrafted ceramics from local artists and artisans are among the arts and crafts you can find at the Sydney Royal Easter Show.

items.title: Angry customers who lost $10m in Zone RV collapse urge ASIC to investigate

children.content: With retirement dreams dashed, customers launch a nationwide campaign for the corporate watchdog to investigate the caravan company's former director over insolvent trading claims.

items.title: Toddler dies after being pulled from water on Queensland property

children.content: An 18-month-old boy has died after being pulled from water at a private property on the Western Downs.

items.title: The cleanest air on Earth holds a powerful lesson about our climate

children.content: Perched on Tasmania's wild north-west coast, Cape Grim has been quietly tracking the world's cleanest air for 50 years. Its data tell an interesting story about how our world has changed over half a century, including rare success stories.

image.altText: Graeme Pearman wears a red jacket and red flares, as he sets up a tower at Cape Grim in 1976

items.title: How one extra tree too close to your house could cost you thousands

children.content: Tree canopy coverage is being boosted in every capital city, but one micro-level analysis has found a link between property prices and the proximity of trees to houses.

image.altText: Dark SUV driving down city street with lots of trees in it

items.title: Bic Runga thought her first album in 15 years might never arrive

children.content: "There were definitely times where I thought I was never going to make anything again," the New Zealand star says of new album Red Sunset.
